title: Dryopteris barteriana
intro: Dryopteris barteriana, also known as Barter's male fern, is a species of fern native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris barteriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dryopteris barteriana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to relieve respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Dryopteris barteriana is classified within the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically, it belongs to the division Pteridophyta. This species is further classified as a member of the class Polypodiopsida and the order Polypodiales. Within the order, it is placed in the family Dryopteridaceae. The genus Dryopteris is a group of ferns commonly known as wood ferns or male ferns. Dryopteris barteriana is a specific species within this genus, known for its unique characteristics and distribution.
